title: US Auto Loan & Dealership Calculator
meta_title: US Auto Loan Calculator | Calculate Out-the-Door Price & Monthly Payment
meta_description: Don't negotiate based on monthly payments. Calculate your true Out-the-Door vehicle price including Dealer Fees, Trade-in Tax Credits, and APR to reveal dealership math.

hero.title: US Auto Loan Calculator
hero.subtitle: Stop negotiating monthly payments at the dealership. Use this precise engine to calculate your absolute Out-the-Door Price, understand the physical impact of Dealer Fees, and measure true Interest cost over the term of your loan.

formula_explanation: Auto loans in the United States operate under aggressive Simple Interest mechanics. Dealerships usually calculate the State Sales Tax on the margin between the New Vehicle Price and your Trade-in value (saving you money on taxes). However, they offset this by injecting non-negotiable 'Dealer Fees' and 'Doc Fees' directly into the cash footprint before compounding strictly across the Loan Term.
example: If you buy a $32,000 truck with a $5,000 Trade-In and $899 in Dealer Fees at 6.5% Sales tax, your Tax burden is just $1,755 (because the trade-in shielded $5k from taxation). Your Out-the-door price spikes to $34,654. After your $3,000 baseline cash down payment, the bank essentially loans you $26,654 over 60 months.

faq.question: Why do dealerships want me to negotiate by the 'Monthly Payment' instead of the Car Price?
faq.answer: Focusing on the monthly payment is the oldest dealership illusion in America. The F&I (Finance and Insurance) office can artificially 'lower' your monthly payment instantly by simply stretching the loan mathematically from 60 months down to 84 months. Your payment drops, but you will pay thousands of extra dollars in physical interest to the bank. Always negotiate strictly the 'Out-the-Door Price'.

faq.question: What is the Trade-In Tax Credit Strategy?
faq.answer: In almost 40 US states, your Trade-in vehicle reduces your taxable footprint. If you are buying a $40,000 car and trading in a $10,000 car, the government only applies the State Sales Tax onto $30,000. This is a massive mathematical advantage to buying at a dealership instead of selling your car privately.

faq.question: Are 'Dealer Fees' or 'Doc Fees' negotiable?
faq.answer: Usually, no, because state laws dictate that if a dealership charges a Doc Fee to one customer, they must charge it universally to avoid discrimination lawsuits. However, you can aggressively negotiate the baseline price of the vehicle itself down by the exact amount of the Doc Fee to perfectly offset the mandatory charge.
